title: Why I don't worry about my cholesterol

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker shares a review from a viewer, discussing the positive effects of adopting an animal-based diet on his family's health. He highlights improvements like reduced postpartum hair loss for his wife and overall vitality for his family, emphasizing their reliance on organ supplements.
chunks.transcript: my entire family is thriving check out this review from devon h on bone marrow and liver and skin hair and nails from heart and soil supplements both my wife and i have been eating animal base for seven months with that we ditched all the supplements and have been exclusively using heart and soil organs my nursing wife also uses skin hair and nails has seen a radical improvement in postpartum hair loss and milk production is on point our eight month old son's first foods have been fruit and organs he's thriving we empty a capsule of bone marrow and liver into some local banana or papaya and he loves it personally i'm on a mission to reverse autoimmune disease and winning the battle with this lifestyle this way of eating and not eating and no social nutrition mahalo to the heart and soil team and paul who is responsible for opening my eyes to the dangers of seed oils which was clearly the root of the problem and my autoimmune issue now i have radical health as a byproduct armed with this knowledge my family is thriving and i can't be more grateful mahalo

chunks.title: Cholesterol Overview
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to cholesterol, where the speaker emphasizes the complexity and nuances surrounding LDL cholesterol and its perceived dangers. He suggests that misconceptions around cholesterol have been perpetuated in mainstream health narratives.
chunks.transcript: and for those of you who don't know the kayla's shirt is coming we've got a new carnival rmd website in the works stay tuned for that but this week's podcast i wanted to do cholesterol 101. cholesterol 101 quickly quickly became cholesterol 201 301 and 401 it's just impossible to talk about cholesterol and ldl and not get complicated but i wanted to start with some real basics and then try and lay out as simply as possible why i don't believe my my personal elevated quote unquote ldl is a problem and why you might reconsider your doctor's suggestion that your elevated quote unquote ldl is a problem if you are insulin sensitive i talk about how to be insulin sensitive and i lay it all out in this podcast so enjoy this podcast on cholesterol 101 this is the single most asked question i get so i want to keep doing education on this the feedback i've gotten in the past is that it's too complicated i'm trying to distill it but it's a complex issue guys so hopefully this is helpful to you all if you guys want to hear from me every week check out my website currently which is the old version it's a little clunky but carnivoremd.com you can sign up for the newsletter there and you will hear about all the podcasts and all the interesting stuff you can follow me on instagram carnivoremd 2.0 or that's really the place to do it tik tok i suppose obviously youtube i'm there as well so without further ado on to the podcast love you all if you repeat a lie often enough it becomes the truth this is of course a quote from joseph goebbels a member of the third reich a nazi propaganda minister unfortunately said that but it rings true today and unfortunately to me it appears to ring true in nutritional science as well as many other parts of our zeitgeist of what we

chunks.title: The Role of LDL in the Body
chunks.summary: The speaker explains the essential role of LDL cholesterol in the body, including its functions in hormone production and cell membrane integrity. He introduces the homeobiscus model, theorizing how dietary fat intake may influence LDL levels adaptively.

chunks.title: Critique of Nutritional Beliefs
chunks.summary: The speaker critiques established beliefs about food and health, particularly regarding red meat and dietary fats. He challenges the idea that these foods are harmful, citing the benefits of an animal-based diet and the dangers of processed foods.

chunks.title: Insulin Resistance and Atherosclerosis
chunks.summary: The conversation delves into the relationship between insulin resistance and cardiovascular health, suggesting that insulin sensitivity is crucial in assessing cardiovascular risk. The speaker argues that elevated LDL is not inherently harmful if one is insulin sensitive.

chunks.title: Empirical Evidence and Case Studies
chunks.summary: The speaker references various medical studies and case reports that demonstrate instances of atherosclerosis occurring without elevated LDL levels, reinforcing his argument against the traditional view of cholesterol as the sole culprit in heart disease.

chunks.title: Personal Insights on Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker shares personal anecdotes regarding his health and dietary practices, emphasizing the importance of avoiding seed oils and processed sugars while consuming nutrient-dense foods. He discusses his own LDL levels and experiences with insulin sensitivity.
